search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae red eye discharge contact lens photophobia guideline
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Commercial and non-official algorithms were not accepted.
population_applicability: Direct for adults and children with conjunctivitis or red-eye complaints, with serious, contact-lens, trauma, and atypical qualifiers retained.
setting_applicability: Direct for DHA-licensed telehealth; partial for in-person fluorescein, full ophthalmic examination, or culture.
UAE_applicability: Verified for DHA-licensed telehealth in Dubai, United Arab Emirates.
recency_verification: The current DHA Issue 2 document, effective 2024-04-21 and due for revision 2029-02-21, was reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: No newer official DHA conjunctivitis issue was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped conjunctivitis, redness, discharge, crusting, itch, swelling, exposure, contact-lens, and duration fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Describes unilateral or bilateral redness, watery, mucus or purulent discharge, crusting, itching, burning, grittiness, foreign-body sensation, contact spread, dry-eye symptoms, and mechanical or chemical insult context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped vision, severe-pain, photophobia, trauma, contact-lens, and corneal warning fields as prompts only.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-assessed visual-acuity, discharge, eyelid, conjunctival, corneal, pupil, history-draft, and qualified swab fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped eye-hygiene, contact-lens, medicine-plan, return, response-review, and follow-up documentation without doses.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led management, contact-lens hygiene or cessation discussion, response review, infection-control education, and referral for persistent symptoms, reduced acuity, photophobia, corneal opacity, painful red eye, severe headache with nausea, hyphema, trauma, or inability to open the eye; dose tables are not mapped.

unresolved_source_gaps: The broad alias eye infection, external-eye examination, fluorescein, eye movements, red reflex, fundoscopy, visual fields, general patient-question field, and autonomous interpretation remain unsupported.
Conjunctivitis subtype, infection, keratitis, trauma, or corneal disease must not be inferred from individual symptoms.
Medication choice or dose, referral urgency, and treatment remain clinician responsibilities.
